why.docContent:
> ## Documentation Index
> Fetch the complete documentation index at: https://bun.com/docs/llms.txt
> Use this file to discover all available pages before exploring further.

# bun why

> Explain why a package is installed

The `bun why` command explains why a package is installed in your project by showing the dependency chain that led to its installation.

## Usage

```bash terminal icon="terminal" theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}}
bun why <package>
```

## Arguments

* `<package>`: The name of the package to explain. Supports glob patterns like `@org/*` or `*-lodash`.

## Options

* `--top`: Show only the top-level dependencies instead of the complete dependency tree.
* `--depth <number>`: Maximum depth of the dependency tree to display.

## Examples

Check why a specific package is installed:

```bash terminal icon="terminal" theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}}
bun why react
```

```txt theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}}
react@18.2.0
  └─ my-app@1.0.0 (requires ^18.0.0)
```

Check why all packages with a specific pattern are installed:

```bash terminal icon="terminal" theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}}
bun why "@types/*"
```

```txt theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}}
@types/react@18.2.15
  └─ dev my-app@1.0.0 (requires ^18.0.0)

@types/react-dom@18.2.7
  └─ dev my-app@1.0.0 (requires ^18.0.0)
```

Show only top-level dependencies:

```bash terminal icon="terminal" theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}}
bun why express --top
```

```txt theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}}
express@4.18.2
  └─ my-app@1.0.0 (requires ^4.18.2)
```

Limit the dependency tree depth:

```bash terminal icon="terminal" theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}}
bun why express --depth 2
```

```txt theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}}
express@4.18.2
  └─ express-pollyfill@1.20.1 (requires ^4.18.2)
     └─ body-parser@1.20.1 (requires ^1.20.1)
     └─ accepts@1.3.8 (requires ^1.3.8)
        └─ (deeper dependencies hidden)
```

## Understanding the Output

The output shows:

* The package name and version being queried
* The dependency chain that led to its installation
* The type of dependency (dev, peer, optional, or production)
* The version requirement specified in each package's dependencies

For nested dependencies, the command shows the complete dependency tree by default, with indentation indicating the relationship hierarchy.

init.docContent:
> ## Documentation Index > Fetch the complete documentation index at: https://bun.com/docs/llms.txt > Use this file to discover all available pages before exploring further. # bun init > Scaffold an empty Bun project with the interactive `bun init` command Get started with Bun by scaffolding a new project with `bun init`. ```bash terminal icon="terminal" theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}} bun init my-app ``` ```txt theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}} ? Select a project template - Press return to submit. ❯ Blank React Library ✓ Select a project template: Blank + .gitignore + CLAUDE.md + .cursor/rules/use-bun-instead-of-node-vite-npm-pnpm.mdc -> CLAUDE.md + index.ts + tsconfig.json (for editor autocomplete) + README.md ``` Press `enter` to accept the default answer for each prompt, or pass the `-y` flag to auto-accept the defaults. *** `bun init` scaffolds a new project with Bun. It infers settings with sane defaults and is non-destructive when run multiple times. ![Demo](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/709451/183006613-271960a3-ff22-4f7c-83f5-5e18f684c836.gif) It creates: * a `package.json` file with a name that defaults to the current directory name * a `tsconfig.json` file or a `jsconfig.json` file, depending if the entry point is a TypeScript file or not * an entry point which defaults to `index.ts` unless any of `index.{tsx, jsx, js, mts, mjs}` exist or the `package.json` specifies a `module` or `main` field * a `README.md` file AI Agent rules (disable with `$BUN_AGENT_RULE_DISABLED=1`): * a `CLAUDE.md` file when Claude CLI is detected (disable with `CLAUDE_CODE_AGENT_RULE_DISABLED` env var) * a `.cursor/rules/*.mdc` file to guide [Cursor AI](https://cursor.sh) to use Bun instead of Node.js and npm when Cursor is detected If you pass `-y` or `--yes`, it will assume you want to continue without asking questions. At the end, it runs `bun install` to install `@types/bun`. *** ## CLI Usage ```bash terminal icon="terminal" theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}} bun init ``` ### Initialization Options {" "} Accept all default prompts without asking questions. Alias: -y{" "} {" "} Only initialize type definitions (skip app scaffolding). Alias: -m{" "} ### Project Templates {" "} Scaffold a React project. When used without a value, creates a baseline React app.
Accepts values for presets:{" "}
    {" "}
  • tailwind – React app preconfigured with Tailwind CSS
    • {" "}
  • shadcn – React app with @shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS
    • {" "}
{" "} Examples:{" "} 
    bun init --react bun init --react=tailwind bun init --react=shadcn
{" "} ### Output & Files {" "} Initializes project files and configuration for the chosen options (e.g., creating essential config files and a starter directory structure). Exact files vary by template.{" "} ### Global Configuration & Context {" "} Run bun init as if started in a different working directory (useful in scripts).{" "} ### Help {" "} Print this help menu. Alias: -h{" "} ### Examples * Accept all defaults ```bash terminal icon="terminal" theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}} bun init -y ``` * React ```bash terminal icon="terminal" theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}} bun init --react ``` * React + Tailwind CSS ```bash terminal icon="terminal" theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}} bun init --react=tailwind ``` * React + @shadcn/ui ```bash terminal icon="terminal" theme={"theme":{"light":"github-light","dark":"dracula"}} bun init --react=shadcn ```

globalFlags.name: silent
globalFlags.description: Don't print the script command

globalFlags.name: elide-lines
globalFlags.description: Number of lines of script output shown when using --filter (default: 10). Set to 0 to show all lines.

globalFlags.name: version
globalFlags.description: Print version and exit

globalFlags.name: revision
globalFlags.description: Print version with revision and exit

globalFlags.name: filter
globalFlags.description: Run a script in all workspace packages matching the pattern

globalFlags.name: bun
globalFlags.description: Force a script or package to use Bun's runtime instead of Node.js (via symlinking node)

globalFlags.name: no-orphans
globalFlags.description: Exit when the parent process dies, and on exit SIGKILL every descendant. Linux/macOS only.

globalFlags.name: shell
globalFlags.description: Control the shell used for package.json scripts. Supports either 'bun' or 'system'

globalFlags.name: workspaces
globalFlags.description: Run a script in all workspace packages (from the "workspaces" field in package.json)

globalFlags.name: parallel
globalFlags.description: Run multiple scripts concurrently with Foreman-style output

globalFlags.name: sequential
globalFlags.description: Run multiple scripts sequentially with Foreman-style output

globalFlags.name: no-exit-on-error
globalFlags.description: Continue running other scripts when one fails (with --parallel/--sequential)

globalFlags.name: watch
globalFlags.description: Automatically restart the process on file change

globalFlags.name: hot
globalFlags.description: Enable auto reload in the Bun runtime, test runner, or bundler

globalFlags.name: no-clear-screen
globalFlags.description: Disable clearing the terminal screen on reload when --hot or --watch is enabled

globalFlags.name: smol
globalFlags.description: Use less memory, but run garbage collection more often

globalFlags.name: preload
globalFlags.description: Import a module before other modules are loaded

globalFlags.name: require
globalFlags.description: Alias of --preload, for Node.js compatibility

globalFlags.name: import
globalFlags.description: Alias of --preload, for Node.js compatibility

globalFlags.name: inspect
globalFlags.description: Activate Bun's debugger

globalFlags.name: inspect-wait
globalFlags.description: Activate Bun's debugger, wait for a connection before executing

globalFlags.name: inspect-brk
globalFlags.description: Activate Bun's debugger, set breakpoint on first line of code and wait

globalFlags.name: cpu-prof
globalFlags.description: Start CPU profiler and write profile to disk on exit

globalFlags.name: cpu-prof-name
globalFlags.description: Specify the name of the CPU profile file

globalFlags.name: cpu-prof-dir
globalFlags.description: Specify the directory where the CPU profile will be saved

globalFlags.name: cpu-prof-md
globalFlags.description: Output CPU profile in markdown format (grep-friendly, designed for LLM analysis)

globalFlags.name: cpu-prof-interval
globalFlags.description: Specify the sampling interval in microseconds for CPU profiling (default: 1000)

globalFlags.name: heap-prof
globalFlags.description: Generate V8 heap snapshot on exit (.heapsnapshot)

globalFlags.name: heap-prof-name
globalFlags.description: Specify the name of the heap profile file

globalFlags.name: heap-prof-dir
globalFlags.description: Specify the directory where the heap profile will be saved

globalFlags.name: heap-prof-md
globalFlags.description: Generate markdown heap profile on exit (for CLI analysis)

globalFlags.name: if-present
globalFlags.description: Exit without an error if the entrypoint does not exist

globalFlags.name: no-install
globalFlags.description: Disable auto install in the Bun runtime

globalFlags.name: install
globalFlags.description: Configure auto-install behavior. One of "auto" (default, auto-installs when no node_modules), "fallback" (missing packages only), "force" (always).

globalFlags.name: i
globalFlags.description: Auto-install dependencies during execution. Equivalent to --install=fallback.

globalFlags.name: eval
globalFlags.description: Evaluate argument as a script

globalFlags.name: print
globalFlags.description: Evaluate argument as a script and print the result

globalFlags.name: prefer-offline
globalFlags.description: Skip staleness checks for packages in the Bun runtime and resolve from disk

globalFlags.name: prefer-latest
globalFlags.description: Use the latest matching versions of packages in the Bun runtime, always checking npm

globalFlags.name: port
globalFlags.description: Set the default port for Bun.serve

globalFlags.name: conditions
globalFlags.description: Pass custom conditions to resolve

globalFlags.name: fetch-preconnect
globalFlags.description: Preconnect to a URL while code is loading

globalFlags.name: experimental-http2-fetch
globalFlags.description: Offer h2 in fetch() TLS ALPN. Same as BUN_FEATURE_FLAG_EXPERIMENTAL_HTTP2_CLIENT=1

globalFlags.name: experimental-http3-fetch
globalFlags.description: Honor Alt-Svc: h3 in fetch() and upgrade to HTTP/3. Same as BUN_FEATURE_FLAG_EXPERIMENTAL_HTTP3_CLIENT=1

globalFlags.name: max-http-header-size
globalFlags.description: Set the maximum size of HTTP headers in bytes. Default is 16KiB

globalFlags.name: dns-result-order
globalFlags.description: Set the default order of DNS lookup results. Valid orders: verbatim (default), ipv4first, ipv6first

globalFlags.name: experimental-stream-iter
globalFlags.description: Enable the experimental stream/iter API (node:stream/iter, node:zlib/iter).

globalFlags.name: expose-gc
globalFlags.description: Expose gc() on the global object. Has no effect on Bun.gc().

globalFlags.name: no-deprecation
globalFlags.description: Suppress all reporting of the custom deprecation.

globalFlags.name: throw-deprecation
globalFlags.description: Determine whether or not deprecation warnings result in errors.

globalFlags.name: title
globalFlags.description: Set the process title

globalFlags.name: zero-fill-buffers
globalFlags.description: Boolean to force Buffer.allocUnsafe(size) to be zero-filled.

globalFlags.name: use-system-ca
globalFlags.description: Use the system's trusted certificate authorities

globalFlags.name: use-openssl-ca
globalFlags.description: Use OpenSSL's default CA store

globalFlags.name: use-bundled-ca
globalFlags.description: Use bundled CA store

globalFlags.name: redis-preconnect
globalFlags.description: Preconnect to $REDIS_URL at startup

globalFlags.name: sql-preconnect
globalFlags.description: Preconnect to PostgreSQL at startup

globalFlags.name: no-addons
globalFlags.description: Throw an error if process.dlopen is called, and disable export condition "node-addons"

globalFlags.name: unhandled-rejections
globalFlags.description: One of "strict", "throw", "warn", "none", or "warn-with-error-code"

globalFlags.name: console-depth
globalFlags.description: Set the default depth for console.log object inspection (default: 2)

globalFlags.name: user-agent
globalFlags.description: Set the default User-Agent header for HTTP requests

globalFlags.name: cron-title
globalFlags.description: Title for cron execution mode

globalFlags.name: cron-period
globalFlags.description: Cron period for cron execution mode

globalFlags.name: main-fields
globalFlags.description: Main fields to lookup in package.json. Defaults to --target dependent

globalFlags.name: preserve-symlinks
globalFlags.description: Preserve symlinks when resolving files

globalFlags.name: preserve-symlinks-main
globalFlags.description: Preserve symlinks when resolving the main entry point

globalFlags.name: extension-order
globalFlags.description: Defaults to: .tsx,.ts,.jsx,.js,.json

globalFlags.name: tsconfig-override
globalFlags.description: Specify custom tsconfig.json. Default $cwd/tsconfig.json

globalFlags.name: define
globalFlags.description: Substitute K:V while parsing, e.g. --define process.env.NODE_ENV:"development". Values are parsed as JSON.

globalFlags.name: drop
globalFlags.description: Remove function calls, e.g. --drop=console removes all console.* calls.

globalFlags.name: feature
globalFlags.description: Enable a feature flag for dead-code elimination, e.g. --feature=SUPER_SECRET

globalFlags.name: loader
globalFlags.description: Parse files with .ext:loader, e.g. --loader .js:jsx. Valid loaders: js, jsx, ts, tsx, json, toml, text, file, wasm, napi

globalFlags.name: no-macros
globalFlags.description: Disable macros from being executed in the bundler, transpiler and runtime

globalFlags.name: jsx-factory
globalFlags.description: Changes the function called when compiling JSX elements using the classic JSX runtime

globalFlags.name: jsx-fragment
globalFlags.description: Changes the function called when compiling JSX fragments

globalFlags.name: jsx-import-source
globalFlags.description: Declares the module specifier to be used for importing the jsx and jsxs factory functions. Default: "react"

globalFlags.name: jsx-runtime
globalFlags.description: "automatic" (default) or "classic"

globalFlags.name: jsx-side-effects
globalFlags.description: Treat JSX elements as having side effects (disable pure annotations)

globalFlags.name: ignore-dce-annotations
globalFlags.description: Ignore tree-shaking annotations such as @__PURE__

globalFlags.name: env-file
globalFlags.description: Load environment variables from the specified file(s)

globalFlags.name: no-env-file
globalFlags.description: Disable automatic loading of .env files

globalFlags.name: cwd
globalFlags.description: Absolute path to resolve files & entry points from. This just changes the process' cwd.

globalFlags.name: config
globalFlags.description: Specify path to Bun config file. Default $cwd/bunfig.toml

globalFlags.name: help
globalFlags.description: Display this menu and exit

bareCommand.description: Run JavaScript/TypeScript files directly or access package scripts and binaries
